T0055.title: 7-14 Payday Trap
T0055.definition: A form of short-term, high-interest lending in credit fraud scenarios, typically involving 7- or 14-day loan terms characterized by exorbitant upfront interest deductions and late fees. "Upfront interest deduction" refers to the practice of deducting a portion of the principal as interest at the time of loan issuance; for example, a borrower might receive only 1,050 yuan on a 1,400 yuan loan, with the deducted amount being the upfront interest.
